claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to know about your child (or children) for whom you're claiming Child Benefit.

**First question: How old is your child?**

The eligibility rules differ based on age:
- **Under 16**: Generally qualifies automatically (subject to other checks)
- **Age 16-19**: May qualify if they're in approved education or training
- **Age 20 or over**: Generally not eligible

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, let me know about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible. I have two children - Alex who is 5 years old, and Blake who is 10 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for that information. You have two children:
- **Alex, age 5**
- **Blake, age 10**

Both children are under 16, which is a good start. Let me continue with the assessment for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are either Alex or Blake working 24 hours or more per week in paid work?**

Given their ages (5 and 10), I expect the answer is no, but I need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not working at all. They're just kids - Alex is only 5 and Blake is 10, so they're both still in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, that's what I expected! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are either Alex or Blake receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This would include benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit claimed in their own name (not as part of your household claim).

Again, given their young ages, I expect the answer is no, but I need to verify this.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them receives any benefits in their own name. They're too young for that sort of thing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are either Alex or Blake in any type of apprenticeship?**

Given that they're 5 and 10 years old and in primary school, I expect they're not in apprenticeships, but I need to confirm this.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not in any apprenticeships. They're just in regular primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Now let me move on to questions about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to ask about your residency status:**

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

If you do, that's straightforward. However, there are also other ways to meet the residency requirements, such as:
- Having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- Having pre-settled status (with certain conditions like working, studying, or having sufficient resources)
- Living abroad as a Crown servant

Please let me know your situation regarding UK residency.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you're living abroad, let me ask some follow-up questions to see if you might still qualify under special circumstances.

**Are any of the following true for you?**

1. **Are you a Crown servant** (or married to/in a civil partnership with a Crown servant)? This includes:
   - Civil servants working for the UK government abroad
   - Members of HM Forces serving abroad

2. **Do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

3. **Do you have pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?** If yes, there are several ways you might still qualify:
   - You're working and earning above the National Insurance threshold
   - You're actively looking for work (up to 91 days)
   - You have sufficient resources to support yourself and your family
   - You're studying with sufficient resources
   - You're a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside

Please let me know if any of these situations apply to you.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those situations apply to me. I'm not a Crown servant, and I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Based on the information you've provided, let me navigate to the outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## **Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Result**

I'm sorry, but based on your circumstances, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake.

### **Summary for Each Child:**

**Alex (age 5):**
- ✓ Meets all child-related criteria (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in apprenticeship)
- ✗ **Cannot claim due to residency requirements not being met**

**Blake (age 10):**
- ✓ Meets all child-related criteria (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in apprenticeship)
- ✗ **Cannot claim due to residency requirements not being met**

### **Why You're Not Eligible:**

While both of your children meet all the child-related criteria, **you do not meet the UK residency requirements** necessary to claim Child Benefit. To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, **OR**
- Meet special circumstances such as:
  - Being a Crown servant (or married to/in civil partnership with one)
  - Having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
  - Having pre-settled status with qualifying criteria (working, studying with resources, etc.)

Since you live abroad and none of these special circumstances apply to you, you cannot claim Child Benefit at this time.

### **What You Could Do:**

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move back to the UK or obtain relevant immigration status), you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit at that time.

**Reference:** [UK Government Child Benefit Eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your situation?

reasonings: Alex (age 5) meets the child-related criteria: under 16, not working 24+ hours per week, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements necessary to claim Child Benefit. The claimant lives abroad and does not qualify under any special circumstances (not a Crown servant, no settled or pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme).

Blake (age 10) meets the child-related criteria: under 16, not working 24+ hours per week, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements necessary to claim Child Benefit. The claimant lives abroad and does not qualify under any special circumstances (not a Crown servant, no settled or pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme).
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children (Alex, age 5, and Blake, age 10) who both meet all the child-related eligibility criteria. However, the claim cannot proceed because the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant lives abroad and does not qualify under any of the special circumstances that would allow a claim from outside the UK (such as being a Crown servant or having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme).

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
